metadata.note: Eval 1 run via live delegation subagent (executor + grader). Evals 2-3 remain analytical pending Claude CLI availability.

expectations.text: Response provides all three traditions (Judaism, Christianity, Islam)
expectations.evidence: Table shows all three: Psalm 103:12 (Judaism), Matthew 6:14-15 (Christianity), Az-Zumar 39:53 (Islam)

expectations.text: Response includes a neutral bridging note that does not assert one tradition supersedes another
expectations.evidence: Bridging note: 'all locate forgiveness at the center of what God is' -- structural observation, no hierarchy

expectations.text: Response references or uses the pre-seeded forgiveness theme data
expectations.evidence: Explicitly references 'theme (ID: forgiveness) is one of the 20 pre-seeded comparisons' with exact passages

expectations.text: Response does not use words like 'original', 'fulfills', or 'supersedes'
expectations.evidence: None of the prohibited words appear in the response

expectations.text: Response provides all three traditions (Judaism, Christianity, Islam)
expectations.evidence: All three covered: Micah 7:18 (Judaism), Matthew 6:14 (Christianity), Surah An-Nur 24:22 (Islam)

expectations.text: Response includes a neutral bridging note that does not assert one tradition supersedes another
expectations.evidence: 'they differ in ritual application, they shared a common ethical imperative' -- neutral, acknowledges difference without hierarchy

expectations.text: Response references or uses the pre-seeded forgiveness theme data
expectations.evidence: Different passages used (Micah 7:18, not Psalm 103:12; Surah An-Nur 24:22, not Az-Zumar 39:53). Pre-seeded theme data not accessed.

expectations.text: Response does not use words like 'original', 'fulfills', or 'supersedes'
expectations.evidence: None of the prohibited words appear in the response

notes: Without-skill 0.75 was higher than analytical estimate (0.17) -- LLM has good general interfaith sensitivity but cannot know the pre-seeded passage IDs

expectations.text: Response explains the proportional representation rule correctly
Response cites US population shares from Pew Research Center
Response clarifies that visual dignity is equal regardless of population share
expectations.evidence: [ANALYTICAL] Skill states 'identical visual and structural dignity'

expectations.text: Response explains that all three traditions must always appear in every comparison

notes: [ANALYTICAL] -- live subagent run pending Claude CLI availability
Eval 1 is a LIVE run -- without_skill scored 0.75, higher than analytical estimate (0.17)
Key finding: LLMs have good general interfaith sensitivity -- the real differentiator is the pre-seeded passage IDs, not neutral tone
Eval 1 suggests the 'neutral bridging note' expectation may not discriminate well -- tighten to require ARE-format bridging note in future evals
